headline: Sabah Coral Triangle Initiative Receives Only 18% of Pledged Funding
context: The Coral Triangle Initiative, launched in 2009, has received only 18% of pledged funding from member nations including Malaysia, undermining reef conservation across 6 countries.

cards.big: The Coral Triangle contains 76% of all known coral species.
cards.sub: Malaysia pledged RM42M to the initiative in 2009. Only RM7.6M — 18% — has been disbursed in 16 years.

cards.big: Sabah's coral reefs generate RM1.2B in tourism annually.
cards.sub: Total national investment in reef conservation is RM28M — 2.3% of the revenue reefs generate.

cards.big: CTI's marine protected area target was 10% by 2020.
cards.sub: Malaysia's actual marine protected area coverage is 3.4% — the lowest among CTI member nations.

cards.big: Reef fish biomass in Semporna declined 44% between 2012 and 2024.
cards.sub: Destructive fishing methods persist despite Sabah Parks patrols.

cards.big: Pledging money to save coral reefs and then not paying is not commitment. It is performance.
International conservation commitments without domestic follow-through damage credibility and reefs equally. Malaysia should either fund its pledges or honestly withdraw.
